annotations.label: 8 is composite, not prime nor semiprime

anchor.snippet: 8 is a composite number and the first number which is neither prime nor semiprime

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Nat.Prime` but has no `semiprime` notion nor a stated fact about 8 being the first non-prime/non-semiprime composite.

annotations.label: Mihăilescu's Theorem applied to 8

anchor.snippet: it is the only nonzero perfect power that is one less than another perfect power

annotations.note: Searches for `Mihailescu`/`Catalan's conjecture`/`catalanConjecture` in Mathlib return no matching declarations.

anchor.snippet: 8 is the first proper Leyland number of the form

annotations.label: 8 as Fibonacci perfect cube

anchor.snippet: 8 is a Fibonacci number and the only nontrivial Fibonacci number that is a perfect cube

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Nat.fib` but does not state the theorem that 8 is the only nontrivial Fibonacci cube.

annotations.label: Sphenic numbers have eight divisors

anchor.snippet: Sphenic numbers always have exactly eight divisors

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `sphenic` definition; the divisor-count consequence is not stated.

annotations.label: Octal number system

anchor.snippet: 8 is the base of the octal number system

annotations.note: No occurrence of `octal`/`Octal` in Mathlib; general `Nat.digits` exists but base‑8 isn't singled out.

annotations.label: Octagon

anchor.snippet: A polygon with eight sides is an octagon

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `Octagon` or general polygon definition.

annotations.label: Regular octagon plane-vertex and tiling

anchor.snippet: A regular octagon can fill a plane-vertex with a regular triangle and a regular icositetragon

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theory of regular-polygon plane-vertex configurations or tilings of this form.

annotations.label: Ammann–Beenker tiling

anchor.snippet: The Ammann–Beenker tiling is a nonperiodic tesselation of prototiles

annotations.note: No `Ammann` in Mathlib; only a Delone-set basic file (`Mathlib.Analysis.AperiodicOrder.Delone.Basic`) exists with no tilings.

annotations.label: Octahedron

anchor.snippet: An octahedron is a regular polyhedron with eight equilateral triangles as faces

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `Octahedron` definition (the matches were for unrelated 'octahedron'-free triangulated-category files).

annotations.label: Stella octangula

anchor.snippet: The stella octangula , or eight-pointed star , is the only stellation with octahedral symmetry

annotations.note: No occurrences of `stella` or polyhedral stellations in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: The cuboctahedron , on the other hand, is a rectified cube or rectified octahedron

annotations.label: Octonions

anchor.snippet: The octonions are a hypercomplex normed division algebra that are an extension of the complex numbers

annotations.note: No `Octonion` or `CayleyDickson` construction in Mathlib; only `Quaternion` exists.

annotations.label: Octonions as double cover of SO(8)

anchor.snippet: They are a double cover of special orthogonal group SO(8)

annotations.note: Mathlib has neither octonions nor a `SO(8)` definition nor a Spin(8) double-cover result.

annotations.label: SU(3) adjoint representation and gluons

anchor.snippet: has an eight-dimensional adjoint representation whose colors are ascribed gauge symmetries

annotations.note: Mathlib has general Lie algebra `adjoint` representations but no `SU(3)` or specific 8-dim adjoint description.

annotations.label: Clifford algebra periodicity

anchor.snippet: Clifford algebras display a periodicity of 8

annotations.note: `CliffordAlgebra` and some equivalences are defined, but the mod‑8 Bott-style periodicity statement is not proved.

annotations.label: Lie group E8 rank

anchor.snippet: The Lie group E 8 has rank 8, and is one of 5 exceptional Lie groups

annotations.note: Mathlib has general root-system/Lie-algebra infrastructure but no specific `E8` definition or rank computation.

annotations.label: Smallest non-abelian group with all normal subgroups

anchor.snippet: The order of the smallest non-abelian group whose subgroups are all normal is 8

annotations.note: `QuaternionGroup 2` (Q₈, order 8) is defined but the minimality statement about Hamiltonian/Dedekind groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bott periodicity theorem

anchor.snippet: The Bott periodicity theorem describes the eightfold periodicity of the homotopy groups

annotations.note: Grep for `Bott`/`bottPeriodicity` in Mathlib returns no matches relevant to the periodicity theorem.
